heading: Protection from coercive contracts
label: 6307b Protection from coercive contracts
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior was renumbered section 18 and is classified to .section 10 of Pub. L. 104–272section 6309 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: is a coercive provision described in subparagraph (B) and is for a period greater than 12 months; or

children.label: ii
children.text: is a coercive provision described in subparagraph (B) and the other boxer under contract to the promoter came under that contract pursuant to a coercive provision described in subparagraph (B).

children.label: B
children.text: A coercive provision described in this subparagraph is a contract provision that grants any rights between a boxer and a promoter, or between promoters with respect to a boxer, if the boxer is required to grant such rights, or a boxer’s promoter is required to grant such rights with respect to a boxer to another promoter, as a condition precedent to the boxer’s participation in a professional boxing match against another boxer who is under contract to the promoter.
This subsection shall only apply to contracts entered into after .May 26, 2000

No subsequent contract provision extending any rights or compensation covered in paragraph (1) shall be enforceable against a boxer if the effective date of the contract containing such provision is earlier than 3 months before the expiration of the relevant time period set forth in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Promotional rights under mandatory bout contracts
children.label: b Promotional rights under mandatory bout contracts
children.text: No boxing service provider may require a boxer to grant any future promotional rights as a requirement of competing in a professional boxing match that is a mandatory bout under the rules of a sanctioning organization.

children.heading: Protection from coercive contracts with broadcasters
children.label: c Protection from coercive contracts with broadcasters
children.text: Subsection (a) of this section applies to any contract between a commercial broadcaster and a boxer, or granting any rights with respect to that boxer, involving a broadcast in or affecting interstate commerce, regardless of the broadcast medium. For the purpose of this subsection, any reference in subsection (a)(1)(B) to “promoter” shall be considered a reference to “commercial broadcaster”.
